sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Coda Octopus Group, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:CODA) 32% Jump Shows Its Popularity With Investors
feed.summary: Coda Octopus Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CODA) recently saw its share price jump 32% in a month, reflecting strong investor sentiment and recovering from prior weakness. The company's high P/E ratio of 39.3x, compared to the market average, is attributed to its superior earnings growth and a projected 43% EPS increase in the coming year, significantly outpacing the general market forecast. This suggests investors expect continued strong performance, justifying the higher valuation.

feed.title: Parsons buys defense tech firm Altamira for $375M to boost national security portfolio
feed.summary: Parsons Corp. has acquired Altamira Technologies for $375 million, marking its fourth acquisition in less than a year. This strategic move adds approximately 600 defense and intelligence industry professionals to Parsons' team, significantly boosting its national security portfolio. The acquisition underscores Parsons' ongoing expansion in the defense technology sector.

feed.title: P&G Leadership - Ranya Shamoon
feed.summary: Ranya Shamoon serves as President of Global Feminine Care at P&G, overseeing iconic brands like Always and Tampax and focusing on driving long-term category growth. With over 30 years at P&G, she has held various leadership roles across multiple categories and is recognized for her dedication to innovation and brand building. Shamoon is also noted for her commitment to empowering girls and women, championing initiatives like #LikeAGirl and #EndPeriodPoverty, and fostering inclusive teams.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Earnings: Valuations Remain Stretched, but We’re Appreciative of Recent Progress
feed.summary: Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for Goldman Sachs (GS) stock to $700 from $630, despite current valuations remaining stretched. This adjustment follows Goldman Sachs' strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings, which showed significant growth in investment banking and markets, and improved targets for asset and wealth management. The firm anticipates continued market share growth for bulge-bracket banks like Goldman Sachs, driven by favorable economic conditions and increasing demand from financial sponsors.

feed.title: Concurrent Investment Advisors LLC Lowers Position in First Trust Preferred Securities and Income ETF $FPE

The ETF recently disclosed a monthly dividend of $0.0732, paid in November, and its stock price opened at $18.35 with a 52-week range of $16.77 to $18.51.

feed.title: JPMorgan downgrades Fortive stock to Underweight on growth concerns
feed.summary: JPMorgan downgraded Fortive (NYSE:FTV) from Neutral to Underweight, citing concerns about software fundamentals and a lack of growth catalysts within the company's portfolio, despite raising its price target to $57.00. The investment bank anticipates Fortive's Q4 results to align with consensus, showing slight year-over-year gains in core sales and margins. This downgrade follows strong Q3 2025 earnings for Fortive and recent product advancements, but aligns with Mizuho's similar downgrade due to a slow start to 2026 across several segments.

feed.title: J.M. Smucker stock rating downgraded by Morgan Stanley on valuation concerns
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley downgraded J.M. Smucker (NYSE: SJM) from Overweight to Equalweight and reduced its price target to $105.00 from $115.00, citing valuation concerns and potential emerging competitive risks. Despite the downgrade, the firm still expects J.M. Smucker to outperform peers in organic sales growth, an outlook that is now better reflected in the market. The stock's current trading price is near the new target, following a period of strong outperformance against its peers.

feed.title: B.Riley initiates Knife River stock with Buy rating, sets $92 price target
feed.summary: B.Riley has initiated coverage on Knife River Corp. (NYSE: KNF) with a Buy rating and a $92 price target, indicating a 15% upside from its current trading price of $80.24. The firm believes the stock is currently undervalued by approximately 25% compared to peers, attributing its recent margin compression to temporary factors rather than a loss of pricing power. Knife River, a vertically integrated construction materials company with $3.05 billion in revenue and $463 million EBITDA over the last twelve months, is expected to see EBITDA margin expansion by 2027 as conditions normalize and strategic initiatives take effect.

feed.title: Henderson replaces Squires at Invesco
feed.summary: Samuel Henderson has been promoted to head of EMEA equity trading at Invesco, taking over from Paul Squires who has left the company. Henderson brings nearly 25 years of industry experience, including eight years at Invesco, where he was most recently head trader for EMEA equities. Invesco reported US$2.1 trillion in assets under management in Q3 2025.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOG is Cortland Associates Inc. MO's 9th Largest Position
feed.summary: Cortland Associates Inc. MO reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) by 14.0% in the third quarter, making it their 9th largest holding. Despite this, Alphabet's stock achieved a $4 trillion market cap due to strong AI momentum, including Apple's adoption of Google's Gemini for Siri, and outperformed earnings estimates. However, insider selling, legal challenges regarding AI training data, and operational constraints like data center hookups present potential future headwinds for the company.

feed.title: Davis Capital Management Invests $2.67 Million in Amazon.com, Inc. $AMZN

Analysts generally rate Amazon as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target around $295.96. Recent positive developments include an AWS European Sovereign Cloud launch and a copper supply deal for AI data centers, though legal and regulatory challenges, such as a Saks bankruptcy dispute and an Italian antitrust fine appeal, present some near-term risks.

feed.title: VOYA GLOBAL ADVANTAGE AND PREMIUM OPPORTUNITY FUND & VOYA INFRASTRUCTURE, INDUSTRIALS AND MATERIALS FUND ANNOUNCES PAYMENT OF MONTHLY DISTRIBUTION

This press release provides an estimate of the sources of these distributions, primarily from net investment income and net realized long-term capital gains, noting that actual tax reporting may vary. The Funds also disclosed their average annual total returns and annualized current distribution rates as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: BlackRock's Jewell Says Strong Earnings to Boost European Stocks
feed.summary: Helen Jewell of BlackRock Inc. predicts that Europe's current equity rally will continue, driven by strong corporate earnings, particularly from banks and AI-related sectors. Despite elevated valuations and the market being technically overbought, she sees potential for an additional 8% to 9% gain this year, provided inflation and economic growth remain stable. Her optimism is partly based on accurate past predictions regarding European equities.

feed.title: FHN Q4 Deep Dive: Loan Growth and Deposit Discipline Drive Outperformance
feed.summary: First Horizon (FHN) exceeded Wall Street's revenue and profit expectations in Q4 CY2025, driven by disciplined deposit cost management, expanded lending, and strong commercial and industrial loan performance. The company’s CEO highlighted increased pre-provision net revenue and a 15% return on tangible common equity for 2025. Looking ahead, FHN anticipates mid-single-digit loan growth, prudent expense management, and continued investments in technology and client relations to sustain profitability.

feed.title: Palo Alto’s Firewall Firewall: New DoS Flaw Echoes Prior Zero-Day Chaos
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has released patches for a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) vulnerability (CVE-2026-0227) in its GlobalProtect gateways and portals, which could allow unauthenticated attackers to crash firewalls. This flaw is concerning as it closely mirrors a previous zero-day exploit (CVE-2025-0114) that caused widespread outages. The vulnerability has a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit circulating, urging immediate updates for enterprise users heavily reliant on GlobalProtect for remote access.

feed.title: Blalock Williams LLC Buys New Position in Eli Lilly and Company $LLY
feed.summary: Blalock Williams LLC has acquired a new position of 10,071 shares in Eli Lilly and Company (LLY), valued at approximately $7.68 million, making it the fund's largest holding. Eli Lilly reported strong Q3 earnings, exceeding revenue and EPS expectations, and provided positive FY2025 guidance. Despite growth catalysts like an AI drug-discovery lab with Nvidia and positive analyst coverage, the company faces near-term risks including a GLP-1 lawsuit and an FDA delay for its oral obesity pill.

feed.title: Landmark Bancorp Sets Date for Q4 2025 Earnings Release
feed.summary: Landmark Bancorp (LARK) announced it will release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings after market close on January 28, 2026, and will host a conference call on January 29, 2026, to discuss the results. The company demonstrates transparency with investors through this formal disclosure and call. Currently, the stock has a Hold rating from analysts with a $27.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates LARK as "Outperform" due to strong earnings and attractive valuation.
